{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Service Representative",
  "description": "Ablaze Consultancy is a dynamic and results-driven firm specializing in recruitment, consulting, and leadership training. We are committed to helping organizations build strong teams by connecting them with top talent and providing strategic business solutions that drive growth and excellence.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Representative

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Manage large amounts of incoming phone calls
	Generate sales leads
	Identify and assess customers&rsquo; needs to achieve satisfaction
	Build sustainable relationships and trust with customer accounts through open and interactive communication
	Provide accurate, valid and complete information by using the right methods/tools
	Meet personal/customer service team sales targets and call handling quotas
	Handle customer complaints, provide appropriate solutions and alternatives within the time limits; follow up to ensure resolution
	Keep records of customer interactions, process customer accounts and file documents
	Follow communication procedures, guidelines and policies
	Take the extra mile to engage customers


Requirements


	Track record of over-achieving quota
	Strong phone contact handling skills and active listening
	Familiarity with CRM systems and practices
	Customer orientation and ability to adapt/respond to different types of characters
	Excellent communication and presentation skills
	Ability to multi-task, prioritize, and manage time effectively.


Salary
N130,000 - N150,000 per month.&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interest and qualified candidates should send their CV&nbsp;to:&nbsp;consultancyablaze@mail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Customer Service Representative Job at Ablaze Consultancy




        
    

        
        Ablaze Consultancy is a dynamic and results-driven firm specializing in recruitment, consulting, and leadership training. We are committed to helping organizations build strong teams by connecting them with top talent and providing strategic business solutions that drive growth and excellence.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Representative

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Manage large amounts of incoming phone calls
	Generate sales leads
	Identify and assess customers’ needs to achieve satisfaction
	Build sustainable relationships and trust with customer accounts through open and interactive communication
	Provide accurate, valid and complete information by using the right methods/tools
	Meet personal/customer service team sales targets and call handling quotas
	Handle customer complaints, provide appropriate solutions and alternatives within the time limits; follow up to ensure resolution
	Keep records of customer interactions, process customer accounts and file documents
	Follow communication procedures, guidelines and policies
	Take the extra mile to engage customers


Requirements


	Track record of over-achieving quota
	Strong phone contact handling skills and active listening
	Familiarity with CRM systems and practices
	Customer orientation and ability to adapt/respond to different types of characters
	Excellent communication and presentation skills
	Ability to multi-task, prioritize, and manage time effectively.


Salary
N130,000 - N150,000 per month. 

Method of Application 
Interest and qualified candidates should send their CV to: consultancyablaze@mail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Service Officer - Technical",
  "description": "Adventure Capital Group - Our client is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Officer - Technical

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Key Responsibilities
Customer Engagement &amp; Documentation:


	Record and track customer complaints and service requests with accuracy and professionalism.
	Prepare and share estimates/quotations, ensuring timely communication and approvals.
	Manage repair orders from initiation to closure, ensuring proper documentation and smooth handover to accounts.


Service Agreements &amp; Preventive Maintenance:


	Draft and renew Service Level Agreements (SLAs) in line with customer needs.
	Ensure preventive maintenance schedules are adhered to, reducing downtime and enhancing customer satisfaction.


Reporting &amp; Compliance:


	Maintain Work in Progress and Prospect Reports to support management decision-making.
	Participate actively in safety meetings, training, and emergency response systems.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, safety standards, and customer service protocols.


Key Requirements


	Minimum of a Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering or related disciplines
	Minimum of 3 years&rsquo; experience in customer service or technical support within cooling systems or related fields.
	Strong technical knowledge of cooling products and ability to interpret mechanical/electrical drawings.
	Excellent communication skills (verbal and written) and stakeholder management ability.
	Proven track record of problem-solving and delivering customer satisfaction.


Skills and Competencies:


	Technical expertise in cooling systems.
	Environmental and safety awareness.
	Strong organizational and documentation skills.
	Customer relationship management.
	Problem-solving and initiative.
	Professionalism and attention to detail.

Customer Service Officer - Technical Job at Adventure Capital Group




        
    

        
        Adventure Capital Group - Our client is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Officer - Technical

Job Location: Lagos

Key Responsibilities
Customer Engagement & Documentation:


	Record and track customer complaints and service requests with accuracy and professionalism.
	Prepare and share estimates/quotations, ensuring timely communication and approvals.
	Manage repair orders from initiation to closure, ensuring proper documentation and smooth handover to accounts.


Service Agreements & Preventive Maintenance:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Draft and renew Service Level Agreements (SLAs) in line with customer needs.
	Ensure preventive maintenance schedules are adhered to, reducing downtime and enhancing customer satisfaction.


Reporting & Compliance:


	Maintain Work in Progress and Prospect Reports to support management decision-making.
	Participate actively in safety meetings, training, and emergency response systems.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, safety standards, and customer service protocols.


Key Requirements


	Minimum of a Bachelor’s Degree in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering or related disciplines
	Minimum of 3 years’ experience in customer service or technical support within cooling systems or related fields.
	Strong technical knowledge of cooling products and ability to interpret mechanical/electrical drawings.
	Excellent communication skills (verbal and written) and stakeholder management ability.
	Proven track record of problem-solving and delivering customer satisfaction.


Skills and Competencies:


	Technical expertise in cooling systems.
	Environmental and safety awareness.
	Strong organizational and documentation skills.
	Customer relationship management.
	Problem-solving and initiative.
	Professionalism and attention to detail.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Logistics Advisor III",
  "description": "Nautilus Nigeria Engineering and Construction Limited (NNEC)&#39;s mission is to provide all clients with high quality, cost-effective, on-schedule engineering, project management, inspection and environmental services. NNEC is committed to design and build more efficient, safer, reliable, and environmentally friendly facilities for the betterment of our clients and their communities.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Logistics Advisor III

Job Location:&nbsp;Nigeria
Job type: Full time

Main Functions


	Logistics Advisor provides logistics subject matter expertise. May include aviation, marine, shore base, ground transportation and camp services to deliver safe reliable and cost efficient logistic services.
	Has overall responsibilities to serve as the contact for providing logistical and materials management services and support for its ongoing business, including affiliates where no local services organization or infrastructure exists. Manages the day-to-day logistical business, including, but not limited to, implementing logistics strategies, supervising drilling logistics contractor staff, material control functions, inventory management, and coordinating material movements to offshore or onshore rig locations.&nbsp;


Tasks and Responsibilities
In addition to all Level 1 and 2 tasks and responsibilities, a Level 3:


	Maintain performance-tracking records of supply base operations and logistics source contracts as a tool for efficiency and continuous improvement opportunities
	Supervise, train, mentor and develop skill sets of national logistics and materials management personnel from start-up to affiliate stage of operations
	Ensures that logistics operations are in conformance with all Company environmental, health, and safety requirements: actively supports and participates in the Company SHE Management system
	Gain alignment with Drill Team on base business services needs, objectives and requirements
	Comply with local in-country regulations, and PSC/JOA requirements
	Assure compliance with DOAG, Controls, and OIMS standards and requirements
	Develop and maintain KPI&#39;s and metrics for supported operations
	Periodically perform internal assessment to assure compliance with controls


Skills and Qualifications


	BS in Engineering, Construction Management, or other Technical degree
	Experience in supply and logistics, preferably in international frontier operations
	Experience working in Upstream Operations, preferably Drilling support activities and related materials, equipment and services. Knowledgeable of procedures and requirements, and be capable of incorporating them into working policies and procedures
	Experienced in starting up new operations from the ground up. Ability to quickly and efficiently develop and build a new operation in a truly remote frontier region
	Must be able to demonstrate sound ethical practices in the supervision of local labor, inventory control, oil field accounting, and contract administration
	Demonstrates strong planning, scheduling, coordination and administration skills. Demonstrates strong communication and interpersonal skills to work with Production, Development, Projects, Drilling and Exploration; able to network effectively across organizations.
	Behavioral Skills: adaptability, analytical capability, collaboration, effective communication, innovation
	Functional Skills: SSHE management - operations and maintenance, process planning and mechanical coordination, maintenance management, maintenance - mechanical, logistics, general marine operations
	Offshore rotation to mobile offshore drilling unit (MODU).


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to:&nbsp;infonnec1@nnecltd.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-02",

Logistics Advisor III Job at Nautilus Nigeria Engineering and Construction Limited




        
    

        
        Nautilus Nigeria Engineering and Construction Limited (NNEC)'s mission is to provide all clients with high quality, cost-effective, on-schedule engineering, project management, inspection and environmental services. NNEC is committed to design and build more efficient, safer, reliable, and environmentally friendly facilities for the betterment of our clients and their communities.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Logistics Advisor III

Job Location: Nigeria
Job type: Full time

Main Functions
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Logistics Advisor provides logistics subject matter expertise. May include aviation, marine, shore base, ground transportation and camp services to deliver safe reliable and cost efficient logistic services.
	Has overall responsibilities to serve as the contact for providing logistical and materials management services and support for its ongoing business, including affiliates where no local services organization or infrastructure exists. Manages the day-to-day logistical business, including, but not limited to, implementing logistics strategies, supervising drilling logistics contractor staff, material control functions, inventory management, and coordinating material movements to offshore or onshore rig locations. 


Tasks and Responsibilities
In addition to all Level 1 and 2 tasks and responsibilities, a Level 3:


	Maintain performance-tracking records of supply base operations and logistics source contracts as a tool for efficiency and continuous improvement opportunities
	Supervise, train, mentor and develop skill sets of national logistics and materials management personnel from start-up to affiliate stage of operations
	Ensures that logistics operations are in conformance with all Company environmental, health, and safety requirements: actively supports and participates in the Company SHE Management system
	Gain alignment with Drill Team on base business services needs, objectives and requirements
	Comply with local in-country regulations, and PSC/JOA requirements
	Assure compliance with DOAG, Controls, and OIMS standards and requirements
	Develop and maintain KPI's and metrics for supported operations
	Periodically perform internal assessment to assure compliance with controls


Skills and Qualifications


	BS in Engineering, Construction Management, or other Technical degree
	Experience in supply and logistics, preferably in international frontier operations
	Experience working in Upstream Operations, preferably Drilling support activities and related materials, equipment and services. Knowledgeable of procedures and requirements, and be capable of incorporating them into working policies and procedures
	Experienced in starting up new operations from the ground up. Ability to quickly and efficiently develop and build a new operation in a truly remote frontier region
	Must be able to demonstrate sound ethical practices in the supervision of local labor, inventory control, oil field accounting, and contract administration
	Demonstrates strong planning, scheduling, coordination and administration skills. Demonstrates strong communication and interpersonal skills to work with Production, Development, Projects, Drilling and Exploration; able to network effectively across organizations.
	Behavioral Skills: adaptability, analytical capability, collaboration, effective communication, innovation
	Functional Skills: SSHE management - operations and maintenance, process planning and mechanical coordination, maintenance management, maintenance - mechanical, logistics, general marine operations
	Offshore rotation to mobile offshore drilling unit (MODU).


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: infonnec1@nnecltd.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

Operations Officer Job at Devien Consult Limited




        
    

        
        Devien Consult Limited - Our client is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Officer

Job Location: Lagos Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Summary


	Our client is seeking a highly organized and results-driven Operations Officer to oversee and optimize daily operational activitie
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for ensuring operational efficiency, process improvement, compliance, and seamless coordination across departments


Job Responsibilties
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Oversee day-to-day operational activities to ensure efficiency and productivity
	Develop, implement, and monitor operational policies and procedures
	Coordinate internal teams to ensure smooth workflow and service delivery
	Monitor budgets, track expenses, and prepare operational reports
	Ensure compliance with company policies and regulatory requirements.


Job Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Management, or a related field
	Minimum of 2 - 3 years of experience in operations, administration, or a related role
	Strong understanding of operational processes and business functions
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint).


Salary
N150,000 – N250,000 Monthly.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
